qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated denial-of-service via unbounded multipart form-data parsing on webhook endpoints
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets multipart/form-data parsing for webhook endpoints, not the JavaScript Task Runner sandbox escape described by the advisory.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: OAuth callback authentication bypass enables cross-user credential theft
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets OAuth callback authentication and ownership checks, not the JavaScript Task Runner sandbox escape described by the advisory.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-side request forgery via unrestricted workflow import-from-URL endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets unrestricted server-side URL fetching in the workflow import endpoint, not the JavaScript Task Runner sandbox escape described by the advisory.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described JavaScript Task Runner sandbox escape or an equivalent sandbox boundary bypass. The only sandbox-related final folder is code_node_sandbox, but its status is no_vuln and it is therefore outside the qualifying-finding selection set.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-jjpj-p2wh-qf23 describes sandbox escape in n8n's JavaScript Task Runner by an authenticated workflow creator/editor when task runners are enabled.
multipart_formdata_dos reports unbounded multipart parsing on webhook endpoints.
oauth_callback_bypass reports OAuth callback authentication and credential ownership bypass.
ssrf_workflow_import reports unrestricted axios.get on GET /rest/workflows/from-url.
code_node_sandbox is the only final report discussing Code node or Task Runner sandbox behavior, and its vulnerability_report.json has status no_vuln.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding exists, so Tier1, Tier2, and Tier3 do not apply.
